title: obione tridentata
intro: Obione tridentata, also known as coastal prickly pear, is a type of cactus plant that is native to the coastal regions of California and Oregon in the United States. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's juice, extracted from its stems, is used to make salves and ointments that promote healing, reduce pain, and prevent infection, providing health benefits such as accelerated wound recovery and improved skin health. Herbal preparations of Obione tridentata can be found in various forms, including salves, ointments, and tinctures, which are used topically to harness the plant's medicinal properties.
